ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather record */,
  "date_time" TEXT /* Date and time of the weather record */,
  "is_holiday" INTEGER /* Indicates if the day is a holiday */,
  "humidity" INTEGER /* Relative humidity percentage */,
  "wind_speed" INTEGER /* Wind speed in miles per hour */,
  "visibility_in_miles" REAL /* Visibility in miles */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature in Kelvin */,
  "rain_p_h" INTEGER /* Probability of rain per hour */,
  "snow_p_h" INTEGER /* Probability of snow per hour */,
  "clouds_all" INTEGER /* Percentage of sky covered by clouds */,
  "weather_type" TEXT /* General type of weather */,
  "weather_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of weather conditions */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "dew_point" REAL /* Dew point temperature in Kelvin */,
  "atmospheric_pressure" REAL /* Atmospheric pressure in hPa */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the weather data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "traffic_data" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each traffic record */,
  "date_time" TEXT /* Date and time of the traffic record */,
  "traffic_volume" INTEGER /* Volume of traffic at the specific time */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the traffic record */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Month of the traffic record */,
  "day" INTEGER /* Day of the traffic record */,
  "weekofyear" INTEGER /* Week of the year for the traffic record */,
  "dayofweek" INTEGER /* Day of the week for the traffic record */,
  "hour" INTEGER /* Hour of the day for the traffic record */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to location */,
  "route_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the specific route */,
  "transport_mode" TEXT /* Mode of transportation (road, rail, air) */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to incident */,
  "average_speed" REAL /* Average speed of traffic */,
  "congestion_level" TEXT /* Level of congestion (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_traffic_data_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_traffic_data_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region of the location */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident */,
  "incident_type" TEXT /* Type of incident (e.g., accident, weather disruption) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the incident */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the incident */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the incident */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of user creation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each weather record
Date and time of the weather record
Indicates if the day is a holiday
Wind speed in miles per hour
Percentage of sky covered by clouds
Unique identifier for each traffic record
Date and time of the traffic record
Volume of traffic at the specific time
Week of the year for the traffic record
Day of the week for the traffic record
Hour of the day for the traffic record
Level of congestion (e.g., low, medium, high)
Type of incident (e.g., accident, weather disruption)
Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst)
